title: [Frontend] Migrate recharts 2 → 3 (Dependabot #540)
bug: Review/QA rework: after the recharts 2→3 migration landed, leadership (JD) asked for the dashboard bar charts to use a red/yellow/green (traffic-light) palette. The charts still drew the attendance bars in a single flat accent colour (indigo on the weekday dashboard, the graphType hue on ChurchGraph), so the ticket was sent back for the colour change.
summary: Added a shared **red → yellow → green** palette (`RED_YELLOW_GREEN_PALETTE`, built from the existing `--destructive` / `--warning` / `--success` design tokens so it stays correct in light & dark) and cycle it per bar via recharts 3 `<Cell>` on the **primary** series of both dashboard charts: the attendance series in `TrendSpark.tsx` and the `stat1` series in `ChurchGraph.tsx`. In `ChurchGraph` the palette only applies on the default colouring path — an explicit `stat1Color` override (used by Shepherding Control's projection legend) still wins, so that consumer is unchanged. The secondary series keeps its single accent so multi-series charts stay readable. Following the `code-reviewer` pass I also (a) made the tooltip legend dot for the primary series follow that week's actual palette colour instead of the flat `Bar fill` (it was showing a stale indigo dot over red/yellow/green bars), and (b) tightened the `<Cell>` React key in `TrendSpark` to `point.id ?? index` to avoid a collision on id-less null-week points. New Vitest+RTL tests drive the real recharts 3 renderer and assert the bars' `fill` cycles red→yellow→green and that the `stat1Color` override is still honoured. Builds on the prior recharts-3 migration commit on this branch; the bar-click drill-down and value-label behaviour are untouched (their existing tests still pass).
evidenceNote: tsc --noEmit and vite build are green; the full Vitest suite is 285/286 with the SAME single pre-existing, unrelated failure as baseline (`src/lib/createApolloClient.test.tsx`, a toast-copy assertion in a file this change never touches — confirmed it fails identically with my changes stashed). Before/after visual QA was run against the live dev environment (dev-api-synago.firstlovecenter.com, localhost:3000 which is in the dev CORS allowlist) logged in as the Bacenta Leader test account. Programmatic proof: the rendered bar `fill` attributes on the Home 'Weekly trend' (Weekday) chart are [red, yellow, green, red, …] AFTER vs all-indigo BEFORE; ChurchGraph bars render from the palette too. The bundled `mcp__playwright` browser tool could not launch in this sandbox (ARM64: no `/opt/google/chrome/chrome`, and the MCP pins the `chrome` channel), so screenshots were captured by driving the already-installed Playwright `chromium` binary directly via Playwright's Node API — same login flow, viewports, and DOM assertions. Screenshots are element crops of the chart card so the bar colours are unmistakable; the before shots were taken with the two source files reverted to the committed (pre-colour) version, then my changes restored.

checks.label: vitest full suite (285/286; 1 pre-existing unrelated failure in createApolloClient.test.tsx)
Bar palette cycles red→yellow→green (new unit tests + live dev fills [red,yellow,green,red])
stat1Color override (Shepherding Control) still honoured over the palette
Drill-down unaffected — recharts-3 payload-unwrap onClick tests still pass
Before/after visual QA on dev — attendance bars indigo → red/yellow/green
Tooltip dot follows the per-bar palette colour (code-reviewer Should-Fix resolved)
No Bootstrap classes in touched files

followUps: `RED_YELLOW_GREEN_PALETTE` is defined identically in ChurchGraph.tsx and TrendSpark.tsx — extract to one shared charts/design-system util so the 'standard' dashboard palette can't drift.
The palette cycles by bar position (index % 3), not by value — if leadership actually wants traffic-light bad/ok/good semantics, add threshold-based colouring in a follow-up.
ChurchGraph's `PRIMARY_COLOR_BY_TYPE` (individual-vs-aggregate hue) now only surfaces via the tooltip-dot fallback since bars use the palette — revisit whether that map is still needed.
`CustomLabel.tsx` is dead code (no importers anywhere) — delete in a cleanup ticket.
`createApolloClient.test.tsx` has a pre-existing failing toast-copy assertion unrelated to this change — fix or delete so the suite is fully green.
